prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were calculated using Density Functional Theory (DFT).

**Fukui Reactivity Analysis** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**), PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: built from the user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz`. SCF converged. No warnings reported.

The most electrophilic site (highest f+) is Carbon at index 3 (1-based index 4) with a value of 0.49642.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f-) is Carbon at index 4 (1-based index 5) with a value of 0.10729.

**Fukui Indices (f+, f-, dual descriptor) per atom:**

| 1 | O | 0.47344 | 0.09677 | 0.37667 |

| 8 | C | 0.28914 | 0.00831 | 0.28082 |
